title: Classic Tomato Sauce
intro: Italian-American tomato sauce grew out of Southern Italian cooking brought to the United States in the late 1800s. Immigrant kitchens relied on canned tomatoes, garlic, olive oil, and oregano to build simple sauces that simmered slowly and filled the house with aroma. This version follows that tradition, gently blooming the aromatics in cold oil, softening the tomatoes before blending, then reducing the sauce slowly with onion and herbs until concentrated and balanced.

ingredientGroups.title: Olive Oil and Aromatics
items: 2 tbsp olive oil
2 tsp finely grated garlic
1 tsp dried oregano
Pinch red pepper flakes
Pinch kosher salt

ingredientGroups.title: Tomatoes
items: 1 (28 oz) can whole peeled tomatoes with their juice
Pinch kosher salt

ingredientGroups.title: Onion and Herbs
items: 1 yellow onion, peeled and cut in half
2 basil sprigs
2 sprigs fresh Mediterranean oregano

stepGroups.title: Build the Base
items: Place a medium saucepan or enameled Dutch oven on the stove while it is still cold.
Add the olive oil, grated garlic, dried oregano, red pepper flakes, and a pinch of kosher salt.
Turn the heat to low and allow the aromatics to warm slowly with the oil, stirring occasionally.
Cook about 3 minutes, until the garlic softens and becomes fragrant. Do not let the garlic brown.

stepGroups.title: Add and Soften the Tomatoes
items: Pour in the whole tomatoes and their juice and add another pinch of kosher salt.
Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and simmer about 15 minutes to soften the tomatoes so they blend easily.

stepGroups.title: Blend and Reduce
items: Remove the lid and use a stick blender directly in the pot.
Pulse until the sauce is mostly smooth but still retains a fine tomato texture.
Add the onion halves cut-side down along with the basil and oregano sprigs.
Simmer the sauce uncovered for 60 minutes, keeping it at a gentle simmer rather than a boil.
Stir occasionally as the sauce reduces and thickens.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Sauce
items: Lift out and discard the onion halves, basil, and oregano.
Taste the sauce and adjust the salt as needed after reduction.
